Broadly, reported rates range from 3% to 46%. Several studies cite recurrence between 5% and 20%, while others report 15% to 25%. One center reported a recurrence rate of 27.4% after a median follow-up of 38 months, with lower rates in microadenomas (19%) versus macroadenomas (45%). Protective factors against recurrence include lower immediate post-operative cortisol concentrations and longer duration of glucocorticoid replacement. These findings underscore the need for lifelong monitoring in all patients with Cushing's disease.

The 1 mg DST assesses the normal feedback reaction of the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis; a morning serum cortisol below 50 nmol/L can exclude Cushing's Syndrome. The choice of test should be individualized based on patient characteristics and lifestyle.
